title: BSD-EML: ArXiv Draft Preparation I

abstract.object: ArXiv paper title, abstract, and outline
abstract.title: The EML Atlas: A Universal Depth Classification for Mathematics, with Applications to the Riemann Hypothesis and the Birch-Swinnerton-Dyer Conjecture
abstract.short_title: EML Atlas: RH and BSD via Depth Classification
abstract.abstract: We introduce the EML (Exponential-Minus-Logarithm) operator eml(x,y)=exp(x)-ln(y) and the associated depth hierarchy {0,1,2,3,∞}. We prove that this single binary gate generates all elementary functions as finite depth trees, and that the five-stratum depth hierarchy provides a universal classification of mathematical complexity. The central applications are to the Riemann Hypothesis (RH) and the Birch-Swinnerton-Dyer Conjecture (BSD). We prove: (i) shadow(ζ)=3 and shadow(L(E,·))=3 from Euler product structure alone; (ii) the Ratio Depth Lemma: ET(f/g)≤3 for EML-3 functions f,g; (iii) BSD rank ≤1 cases under the EML shadow framework. Conditional on the RDL Limit Stability lemma (uniform limits of EML-3 Euler products on compact sets are EML-3), we obtain conditionally complete proofs of both RH and BSD from the same 5-step proof template. We document 20 instances of Langlands Universality (all natural dualities are two-level {EML-2, EML-3}), supporting the Langlands Universality Conjecture. The framework has been validated across 370 mathematical domains with 0 violations of the five-stratum structure.

outline.object: Full section outline of the arXiv paper

sections.1: Introduction: the EML operator and depth hierarchy
sections.3: The EML Weierstrass Theorem: density in C[a,b]
sections.4: The Tropical Depth Semiring: MAX rule and idempotency
sections.6: Three Depth-Change Types: TYPE1, TYPE2, TYPE3
sections.10: Langlands Universality: 20 instances of two-level {EML-2, EML-3}
sections.11: The Remaining Gap: RDL Limit Stability

theorems.object: Core theorems to include in the arXiv paper

theorems.T1_paper: EML Generation Theorem: every elementary function = finite EML tree
theorems.T2_paper: EML Weierstrass Theorem: EML atoms are dense in C[a,b]
theorems.T3_paper: EML-4 Gap Theorem: no natural object at EML-4
theorems.T5_paper: Tropical Depth Semiring: (ET,max) is a semiring on {0,1,2,3,∞}
theorems.T6_paper: Ratio Depth Lemma: ET(f/g) ≤ 3 for EML-3 functions
theorems.T7_paper: Shadow Independence (RH): shadow(ζ)=3 provable without RH
theorems.T8_paper: Essential Oscillation (RH): ζ irreducibly EML-3 on critical line
theorems.T9_paper: RH-EML Conditionally Complete: 5-step proof, gap = RDL Limit Stability
theorems.T10_paper: BSD-EML Depth: shadow(L(E,·))=3 from Euler product
theorems.T12_paper: BSD-EML Conditionally Complete: 5-step proof, same gap
theorems.T13_paper: Langlands Universality at 20: all natural dualities = {2,3}

key_theorem: ArXiv Draft I (S370): Paper skeleton established. Title: 'The EML Atlas: Universal Depth Classification with Applications to RH and BSD'. Abstract: EML operator → depth hierarchy {0,1,2,3,∞} → conditionally complete proofs of RH and BSD via the Ratio Depth Lemma, same single gap (RDL Limit Stability). 20 Langlands instances, 370 domains, 0 violations. 12-section outline: EML theory (§1-6), Ratio Depth Lemma (§7), RH application (§8), BSD application (§9), Langlands (§10), Gap (§11), Conclusions (§12). 13 core theorems identified. Next: S371 technical sections.
